description: Dark patterns undermine consumer choice. Session paths that repeatedly bounce users away from the opt-out confirmation page without completion suggest manipulative UXтАФrelevant to CPRA enforcement priorities on ease of exercise of rights.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes web and sourcetypes ux:privacy_flow. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Instrument `step_name` consistently across web and app; (2) exclude A/B tests via `experiment_id` lookup; (3) product design reviews on outliers; (4) correlate with conversion metrics carefullyтАФavoid accidental shaming; (5) legal escalation only on sustained campaigns. Indexes required: `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ux:privacy_flow`.
visualization: Funnel diagram export, Table (sessions), Single value (sessions over threshold). Key fields: `backtrack`, `loop_count`, `session_id`.

references.title: Splunk Lantern тАФ use case library

knownFalsePositives: Access requests from authorized regulatory inquiries, scheduled subject right campaigns, or outreach during privacy reviews.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
тАв Install and configure the required add-on or app: Client-side telemetry HEC (privacy-safe event schema).
тАв Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=web` `sourcetype="ux:privacy_flow"` (session_id, step_name, sequence, _time).
тАв For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 тАФ Configure data collection
(1) Instrument `step_name` consistently across web and app; (2) exclude A/B tests via `experiment_id` lookup; (3) product design reviews on outliers; (4) correlate with conversion metrics carefullyтАФavoid accidental shaming; (5) legal escalation only on sustained campaigns.

Step 2 тАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=web sourcetype="ux:privacy_flow" earliest=-30d
| sort 0 session_id, _time
| streamstats window=1 global=f last(step_name) as prev_step by session_id
| eval backtrack=if(step_name="dns_intro" AND prev_step="dns_confirm",1,0)
| stats sum(backtrack) as loop_count by session_id
| where loop_count>3
| sort - loop_count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA тАФ Dark Patterns тАФ Forced Navigation Loops on тАЬDo Not Sell or ShareтАЭ Choice Screen** тАФ Dark patterns undermine consumer choice. Session paths that repeatedly bounce users away from the opt-out confirmation page without completion suggest manipulative UXтАФrelevant to CPRA enforcement priorities on ease of exercise of rights.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=web` `sourcetype="ux:privacy_flow"` (session_id, step_name, sequence, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Client-side telemetry HEC (privacy-safe event schema). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedтАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: ux:privacy_flow. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

тАв Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype="ux:privacy_flow", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
тАв Orders rows with `sort` тАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
тАв `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by session_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
тАв `eval` defines or adjusts **backtrack** тАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
тАв `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by session_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
тАв Filters the current rows with `where loop_count>3` тАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
тАв Orders rows with `sort` тАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 тАФ Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 тАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Funnel diagram export, Table (sessions), Single value (sessions over threshold).
